info.title: SCIM v2
info.description: Manage your users and groups using SCIM 2.0.

/scim/v2/ServiceProviderConfig.description: Use to facilitate service provider feature discovery

get.description: Returns Talend's configuration details for our SCIM API, including which operations are supported.

/scim/v2/Schemas.description: Talend currently supports schemas for users, groups and roles. Querying the schemas will provide the most up-to-date rendering of the supported SCIM attributes.

get.description: Return Talend's configuration details for how users, groups and roles are formatted.

/scim/v2/Schemas/{id}.description: Talend currently supports schemas for users, groups and roles. Querying the schemas will provide the most up-to-date rendering of the supported SCIM attributes.

get.description: Return Talend’s configuration details for how a specific resource is formatted.

/scim/v2/ResourceTypes.description: Endpoint used to discover the types of resources available.

get.description: Talend currently supports resources users, groups and roles.

/scim/v2/ResourceTypes/{id}.description: Endpoint used to get detail about a specific resource type.

get.description: Talend currently supports resources users, groups and roles.

/scim/v2/Users.description:
As you might expect, users map to the individuals of your account (domain). Each user contains properties called attributes. You can list users, filter by attribute, add new users, update a user's profile information, or remove a user entirely.

Attributes are the details associated with a user's account. These are the details that someone would typically set in their profile. The following table maps SCIM attributes with Talend Cloud user attributes:

| Talend user field | SCIM attribute | Attribute type |Required|
| -------- | -------- | -------- | -------- |
| login     | userName     | Singular     | **Required** |
| firstName     | name.givenName     | Singular     | **Required** |
| middleName     | name.middleName     | Singular     | |
| lastName     | name.familyName     | Singular     | **Required** |
| email     | 	emails\[primary=true\].value     | Multi-Valued     | |
| phone     | phoneNumbers\[type='work'\].value     | Multi-Valued     | |
| active     | active     | Singular     | |
| password     | password     | Singular     | |
| title     | title     | Singular     | |
| preferredLanguage     | preferredLanguage     | Singular   |   |
| timezone     | timezone     | Singular     | |
| groups     | groups     | Multi-Valued     | |
| roles     | roles     | Multi-Valued     | |

Requires the permission **Users - Manage** (**TMC_USER_MANAGEMENT**).

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of users, 100 user per page by default. `count` and `startIndex` parameters can be updated to get larger pages or to retrive other pages.

It is possible to use `filter` parameter to return a list of specific users (see [SCIMv2 specification](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7644#section-3.4.2.2) for details).

It is also possible to sort the results using the parameters `sortBy` and `sortOrder`. The attributes `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` can be set to select or exclude specific attributes.

post.description:
Create and retrieve a user resource.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

/scim/v2/Users/{id}.description:
As you might expect, users map to the individuals of your account (domain). Each user contains properties called attributes. You can list users, filter by attribute, add new users, update a user's profile information, or remove a user entirely.

Attributes are the details associated with a user's account. These are the details that someone would typically set in their profile. The following table maps SCIM attributes with Talend Cloud user attributes:

| Talend user field | SCIM attribute | Attribute type |Required|
| -------- | -------- | -------- | -------- |
| login     | userName     | Singular     | **Required** |
| firstName     | name.givenName     | Singular     | **Required** |
| middleName     | name.middleName     | Singular     | |
| lastName     | name.familyName     | Singular     | **Required** |
| email     | 	emails\[primary=true\].value     | Multi-Valued     | |
| phone     | phoneNumbers\[type='work'\].value     | Multi-Valued     | |
| active     | active     | Singular     | |
| password     | password     | Singular     | |
| title     | title     | Singular     | |
| preferredLanguage     | preferredLanguage     | Singular   |   |
| timezone     | timezone     | Singular     | |
| groups     | groups     | Multi-Valued     | |
| roles     | roles     | Multi-Valued     | |

Requires the permission **Users - Manage** (**TMC_USER_MANAGEMENT**).

get.description:
Retrieve a single user resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

put.description:
Update and return a single user resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

delete.description: Delete a single user resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.

patch.description:
Update partially and retrieve a single user resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

post.description:
Returns a paginated list of users, 100 user per page by default. `count` and `startIndex` parameters can be updated to get larger pages or to retrive other pages.

It is possible to use `filter` parameter to return a list of specific users (see [SCIMv2 specification](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7644#section-3.4.2.2) for details).

It is also possible to sort the results using the parameters `sortBy` and `sortOrder`. The attributes `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` can be set to select or exclude specific attributes.

/scim/v2/Groups.description:
Groups map to Talend groups with the following attributes:
| Talend user field | SCIM attribute | Attribute type |Required|
| -------- | -------- | -------- | -------- |
| name     | displayName     | Singular     | **Required** |
| userIds     | members.value     | Multi-Valued     | |

Requires the permission **Groups - Manage** (**TMC_GROUP_MANAGEMENT**).

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of groups, 100 user per page by default. `count` and `startIndex` parameters can be updated to get larger pages or to retrive other pages.

It is possible to use `filter` parameter to return a list of specific users (see [SCIMv2 specification](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7644#section-3.4.2.2) for details).

It is also possible to sort the results using the parameters `sortBy` and `sortOrder`. The attributes `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` can be set to select or exclude specific attributes.

post.description:
Create and retrieve a group resource.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

/scim/v2/Groups/{id}.description:
Groups map to Talend groups with the following attributes:
| Talend user field | SCIM attribute | Attribute type |Required|
| -------- | -------- | -------- | -------- |
| name     | displayName     | Singular     | **Required** |
| userIds     | members.value     | Multi-Valued     | |

Requires the permission **Groups - Manage** (**TMC_GROUP_MANAGEMENT**).

get.description:
Retrieve a single group resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

put.description:
Update and retrieve a single group resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

delete.description: Delete a single group resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.

patch.description:
Update partially and retrieve a single group resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

post.description:
Returns a paginated list of users, 100 user per page by default. `count` and `startIndex` parameters can be updated to get larger pages or to retrive other pages.

It is possible to use `filter` parameter to return a list of specific users (see [SCIMv2 specification](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7644#section-3.4.2.2) for details).

It is also possible to sort the results using the parameters `sortBy` and `sortOrder`. The attributes `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` can be set to select or exclude specific attributes.

/scim/v2/Roles.description:
Roles map to Talend roles with the following attributes:
| Talend user field | SCIM attribute | Attribute type |Required|
| -------- | -------- | -------- | -------- |
| name     | name     | Singular     | **Required** |
| permissions     | entitlements     | Multi-Valued     | **Required**|

Requires the permission **Roles - Manage** (**TMC_ROLE_MANAGEMENT**).

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of roles, 100 user per page by default. `count` and `startIndex` parameters can be updated to get larger pages or to retrive other pages.

It is possible to use `filter` parameter to return a list of specific users (see [SCIMv2 specification](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7644#section-3.4.2.2) for details).

It is also possible to sort the results using the parameters `sortBy` and `sortOrder`. The attributes `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` can be set to select or exclude specific attributes.

post.description:
Create and retrieve a role resource.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

/scim/v2/Roles/{id}.description:
Roles map to Talend roles with the following attributes:
| Talend user field | SCIM attribute | Attribute type |Required|
| -------- | -------- | -------- | -------- |
| name     | name     | Singular     | **Required** |
| permissions     | entitlements     | Multi-Valued     | **Required**|

Requires the permission **Roles - Manage** (**TMC_ROLE_MANAGEMENT**).

get.description:
Retrieve a single role resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

put.description:
Update and retrieve a single role resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

delete.description: Delete a single user resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.

patch.description:
Update partially and retrieve a single role resource. The value of the {id} should be a UUID.
It is possible to select or exlude attributes using `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` query parameters.

post.description:
Returns a paginated list of roles, 100 user per page by default. `count` and `startIndex` parameters can be updated to get larger pages or to retrive other pages.

It is possible to use `filter` parameter to return a list of specific users (see [SCIMv2 specification](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7644#section-3.4.2.2) for details).

It is also possible to sort the results using the parameters `sortBy` and `sortOrder`. The attributes `attributes` and `excludedAttributes` can be set to select or exclude specific attributes.

401Unauthorized.description: The system failed to authenticate the user. Either the Authorization header was missing or the provided token was incorrect.

403Forbidden.description: The system failed to authorize the user. The provided token was recognized but did not have the rights to perform the action. Contact your security administrator to get the appropriate rights.

400BadRequest.description: Invalid request body. The message varies depending on the cause of the bad request. For example, the cause could be a malformed body or parameter.

429TooManyRequests.description: Too many requests were sent. Check the X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining and X-RateLimit-Reset headers.

500InternalServerError.description: The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.
